title: Family Support Centers/Outreach - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Family Support Centers/Outreach 52 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter At Risk Families Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES FAMILY SUPPORT AND CONNECTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home parenting education that focuse on child development, age-appropriate behavior, parent-child interaction, positive discipline, family health and safety, parenting outreach services, and diapers. 207 NE Park Street Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Must have a dependent in the home under 18 years of age Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4:30pm, Friday 8am-noon Email (541) 426-9411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES BUILDING HEALTHY FAMILIES EASTERN OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT AND CONNECTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home parenting education that focuse on child development, age-appropriate behavior, parent-child interaction, positive discipline, family health and safety, parenting outreach services, and diapers. 2725 7th Street Room 002 Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Must have a dependent in the home under 18 years of age Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4:30pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 524-2331 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator tool with list of Family Resource Centers (FRCs) by school district. Provides a range of services and support to families with children, including child literacy groups and story times, playgroups, assistance with accessing school personnel, parent education, parent literacy and computer support, and other drop-in services for families and children. Some locations may offer children's clothing and other supplies. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY THE BLACK EARLY LEARNING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides developmental screening, referrals to educational and community resources, learning opportunities, connects children with other Black children, and supports parents as they help children learn and grow. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 0-6 African American/Black children Hours: Varies Email (541) 357-5719 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY LEARNING HUB OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY CLACKAMAS EARLY LEARNING HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A network of providers who work to enhance the ability of parents and caregivers to stimulate, protect, and nurture children. In order to lower the incidences of child abuse and neglect, increase effective parenting, and improve family health and well-being via parent education trainings, strengthening linkages among providers, and improving access to support for all families. Coordinated enrollment into subsidized preschool, including Head Start and Preschool Promise. Serves as the Coordinate Early Learning Hub for the county. 821 Main Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-8 Families at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for subsidized preschool enrollment Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (971) 288-8264 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support through home visits, resource navigation, and respite child care for families in immediate need of help. Families involved in outreach also have access to other Relief Nursery services. 1720 W 25th Avenue Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Low-income families with children age 6 and younger who are experiencing multiple stressors Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm (541) 343-9706 111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY ROBIN JAQUA CHILD AND FAMILY CENTER RELIEF NURSERY OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting support through home visits, resource navigation, and respite child care for families in immediate need of help. Families involved in outreach also have access to other Relief Nursery services. 850 S 42nd Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Low-income families with children age 6 and younger who are experiencing multiple stressors Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 8:30am-12:30pm (541) 343-9706 111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS CHELSEA'S PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 2425 Lancaster Drive NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 798-4744 130 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS DORIS'S PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 1601 Monmouth Street Suite #101 Independence, OR 97351 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service (503) 566-2132 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS WOODBURN PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Access to food, diapers and clothing available for families enrolled in the program. 950 N Boone Ferry Road Woodburn, OR 97071 Eligibility: Families with minor children prenatal-age 5 Hours: Varies by service (503) 566-2132 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY DEVELOPMENT CENTER DOUGLAS COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family support programs, in-home support and counseling, crisis intervention, parent education classes and support groups, community resource and referrals, and play therapy for children. Offers classes for young children with trained teachers. 300 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 673-4354 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers comprehensive family support for families with children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Services include therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, family support and individualized case management, home visiting, parenting education, and group activities designed to strengthen parenting skills and promote healthy child development 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Young children ages 0-5 who are experiencing high levels of stress or who have experienced trauma and/or are at risk for abuse and neglect; parents who have multiple risk factors and are struggling to nurture and care for their children. High-risk families typically experience several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parenthood, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, history with drugs and alcohol, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 720 N 14th Street Cottage Grove, OR 97424 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY NORTH DOUGLAS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 239 East B Avenue Drain, OR 97435 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY SOUTH LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides families with a comprehensive set of early childhood and parenting programs, including case management, home visitation, respite care, early intervention, and early childhood special education. Also provides early childhood therapeutic classrooms. Programs for participating parents include a clothing closet, parent education groups, support groups, parent training, a parent resource library, and referrals to child passenger seat programs. 75 S 4th street Creswell, OR 97426 Eligibility: Families with children ages 6 weeks-6 years -Therapeutic classrooms: Ages 6 weeks-4 years -Respite care and other services: Ages 6 weeks-6 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 942-4835 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers information about applying for SNAP (food stamps) and the Oregon Health Plan, subsidized housing, counseling and support groups, and other information about local services and programs. Distributes and accepts applications for the Lions Club Sight and Hearing Program, which provides vision and hearing tests, glasses, and hearing aids to low-income individuals who have no other means of obtaining vision or hearing exams and aids. Distributes and accepts applications for dental clinics for low-income individuals without dental insurance or any realistic way of paying for dental care. 3520 Frontage Road Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-2pm Email (541) 271-9700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY ALBANY CLASSROOM FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Provides high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. Services include parenting education, support through home visits, group activities, and resource referral. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 1055 Bain Street Southeast Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Birth-5 years old Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 967-6580 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief Nurseries serve families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Family Tree Relief Nursery provides high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. Risk factors include: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parent, criminal history, adult victim of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, and social isolation. Services include parenting education, support through home visits, group activities, resource referral. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 1305 Hill Street SE Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Birth-5 years old Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 967-6580 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY LEBANON CLASSROOM FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Provides high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. Services include parenting education, support through home visits, group activities, and resource referral. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 3111 S main Road Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Birth-5 years old Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm (541) 967-6580 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOREST GROVE SCHOOL DISTRICT EARLY CHILDHOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER FAMILY RESOURCE MANAGER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one information and referrals to families about early learning, parenting support, basic needs, and health. Provides information about and hosts play groups, parenting classes, and early learning workshops. In class, parents have an opportunity to interact with each other and boost their parenting skills while learning nurturing strategies to support their children at home and in school. 200 N 14th Avenue Cornelius, OR 97113 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 359-8817 5504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting materials, family events, and a free lending library. It also hosts a variety of classes and groups offered by the agencies within the center. The agencies it houses include the Gladstone Health & Wellness Center, pediatric health, emotional and dental services. Family Stepping Stones, Relief Nursery, Head Start and Early Head Start, Northwest Family services navigators, Clackamas Education Service District EI/ECSE program, LifeWorks Northwest family counseling, WIC, SMART, Clackamas Community College GED program, and the Gladstone School District kindergarten program. 18905 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Households with children from birth through kindergarten Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, also evening hours vary Email (503) 496-3939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES PARENTING RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource closet of baby clothing and toys for enrolled program participants. 4900 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Parents age 22 and younger Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 239-6996 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH & LAKE COMMUNITY ACTION SERVICES FAMILY SUPPORT AND CONNECTIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and referrals, family development support, one-on-one mentoring, and parenting/life skills workshop groups. 2316 South 6th Street Suite B Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Must meet one of the following: -Households receive Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) or Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) -Household has at least one minor child in the home -Co-parenting or having visitation with a minor child -Applicant needs a parenting class certificate Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-4pm, closed all federal holidays Email (541) 882-3500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW GLADSTONE CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nurseries serve families with young children who are most at risk of abuse and neglect. They provide high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. LifeWorks NW provides wraparound services to high-risk families. Services include early childhood intervention and social, emotional, and family support. Also provides mental health services and trauma assessments, traumatic stress expertise, home visitations, parent/infant and parent/toddler classes, art and music therapy, respite child care, and weekly parenting classes using the "Making Parenting a Pleasure" curriculum. Parents/children must be enrolled to receive most services. 18907 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Caregivers with children ages 0-5 and women in their 3rd trimester of pregnancy Serves high-risk families. "High-risk" typically means experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent households, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 496-5770 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW HILLSBORO MAIN STREET CHILDREN'S RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nurseries serve families with young children who are most at risk of abuse and neglect. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 283-4776 Get Directions Visit Website More Details METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading clubs, gender groups, conflict resolution classes, case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 1700 NW Civic Drive Suite 200 Gresham, OR 97080 Eligibility: Grades 5-12 who are at risk of academic failure and their families Hours: Varies Email (971) 506-2609 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PARENTING GROUPS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides childbirth education, newborn care classes, safe sleep workshop, breastfeeding workshop/support, and other classes which vary by season. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Most classes are for parents and other caregivers of children under the age of 5 Hours: Class times vary Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOUNTAINSTAR FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides intensive support through individualized case management, parenting education, home visits, and group activities. Hours: By appointment Email (541) 416-0146 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST FAMILY SERVICES FAMILY SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a wide range of services to individuals and families, including educational seminars and classes on a variety of topics. Programs focus on family stability and child well-being. Supports youth in their daily connections, emphasizing healthy decision-making. Areas of particular focus include drug, alcohol, and gang prevention as well as positive mental health strategies, including suicide prevention. 6200 SE King Road Portland, OR 97222 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 546-6377 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OLD MILL CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief nursery providing services for at-risk families that include therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers, home visiting, outreach and parent support groups. Resource closet available with food, children's clothing, diapers, wipes, hygiene items and cleaning supplies. 1650 SW 45th Place Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 who are low-income, a single-parent, teen parent, experiencing mental health or drug/alcohol issues, victims of trauma or domestic violence, involvement with child welfare, homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 757-8068 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPHELIA'S PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps girls make healthy life choices through empowerment, education, and support. Provides on-site after-school programs and individual, family, and group therapy. Also provides services in schools, including classroom presentations, girl support groups, and staff training in the areas of healthy relationships, bullying and harassment, healthy body image, and digital safety. Offers professional development training for people-serving organizations, nonprofits, and businesses. 1577 Pearl Street Suite 100 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Ages 10-18 who identify as female Hours: By appointment: Monday-Thursday 10am-6pm After School Drop-in hours: Monday-Thursday 3pm-6pm Email (541) 284-4333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Therapeutic classrooms for toddlers and preschoolers and home visits provided as well. 1312 SW 2nd Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Parents experiencing stress with children ages 0-6 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 215-1017 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHERWOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER SHARE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support for families in need. Offers the use of computers and office equipment for job search and accessing community resources. Gives referrals to parenting classes. 21920 SW Sherwood Boulevard Sherwood School District Office Sherwood, OR 97140 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm, during the school year. Summer hours by appointment only. Mental health care, counseling for domestic violence, and support for adults recovering from addiction are offered as well. 780 SE 6th Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: -High-risk families with children ages 0-5. Families with children younger than 18 months are served through home visits only -High-risk typically means families experiencing several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single-parent household, criminal history, adult survivor of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Classroom sessions: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday.
